post.desc:
For context, it is theorized that Chimata is Kamuōichihime, who would also be another sister along with the well-known Konohanasakuyahime and Iwanagahime. 🤔 Though do keep in mind that the line is really thin, with most of the foundation being just the fact that Chimata is an ichigami.

A "market god" is not something exclusive to Kamuōichihime. There are a lot of kamis who are also ichigami – famous names like Daikoku-sama revered by the Earth Rabbits, Ebisu, Inari, Jizō... Despite that, however, I still hold my candle for the theory. I do love the prospect that **both of Sakuya's older sisters have revealed themselves in Gensōkyō.**

Another thing would be the [**Hare and Ke.**](https://kuon.tokyo/blogs/blog/hare-and-ke) Fan translations seem to have gone with Chimata being a "god of revelry", which I personally think it's a bit off. Hare and Ke are specific concepts in Japanese – the special and the mundane. Hence, I believe transliterating it like Yin and Yang would be more appropriate.
